cat /etc/init.d/cron:
#!/bin/sh
### BEGIN INIT INFO
# Provides:          cron
# Required-Start:    $local_fs $remote_fs
# Required-Stop:     $local_fs $remote_fs
# Default-Start:     2 3 4 5
# Default-Stop:      0 1 6
# Short-Description: Cron Daemon
# Description:       This file starts and stops the cron daemon.
### END INIT INFO

# PATH should be set to ensure we can find the cron executable
PATH=/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin

DAEMON=/usr/sbin/cron
NAME=cron
DESC="cron daemon"

test -x $DAEMON || exit 0

case "$1" in
  start)
    echo -n "Starting $DESC: "
    start-stop-daemon --start --quiet --pidfile /var/run/cron.pid --make-pidfile --background --exec $DAEMON
    echo "$NAME"
    ;;
  stop)
    echo -n "Stopping $DESC: "
    start-stop-daemon --stop --quiet --pidfile /var/run/cron.pid
    echo "$NAME"
    ;;
  restart|force-reload)
    $0 stop
    $0 start
    ;;
  status)
    if [ -f /var/run/cron.pid ]; then
      echo "$NAME is running"
    else
      echo "$NAME is not running"
    fi
    ;;
  *)
    echo "Usage: $0 {start|stop|restart|force-reload|status}"
    exit 1
    ;;
esac

exit 0
